methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Quantum Computing
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Quantum Computing
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Quantum Computing

data.narrative:
Nord Quantique is a seed-stage quantum computing company that has raised C$40M+ in total funding, including a C$23M allocation from the Quantum Champions initiative in December 2025. The quantum computing sector comprises a comp universe of 3 public companies (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private peers, with top-tier private comparables including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion.

A peer-median valuation approach is not applicable here due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage datapoints within the quantum computing sector. The analysis identified 7 same-sector peers but none satisfied the narrow filtering criteria of matching stage, adjacency, or valuation tier needed for a robust comparable company analysis—the threshold for statistical reliability is a minimum of 3 aligned datapoints. Falling back to an all-sector median would introduce material distortion given the heterogeneity of business models and capital intensity across quantum platforms.

Without a triangulated analysis supported by sufficient comparable data, a single-method valuation cannot be responsibly derived. The key uncertainty is the absence of a sufficiently dense peer set at seed stage in quantum computing. Further analysis would require either (1) expansion to adjacent early-stage deep-tech sectors, (2) bottom-up DCF modeling contingent on technical and commercialization milestones, or (3) additional seed-stage quantum financing data to establish valuation anchors.
